title: Fagara articulata
intro: Fagara articulata, also known as Zanthoxylum articulatum, is a species of shrub or small tree in the family Rutaceae, native to tropical Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and analgesic effects, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as arthritis and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Fagara articulata include limonoids, alkaloids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Fagara articulata is commonly used in traditional medicine and can be found in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of ailments and to promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Fagara articulata is classified within the Rutaceae family and the genus Fagara. The Rutaceae family, also known as the citrus family, consists of flowering plants that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Fagara is native to Africa and southern Asia, and it is characterized by its fragrant leaves and small, white flowers. Fagara articulata, specifically, is a species of shrub or small tree that is native to tropical Africa. Through its classification, we can understand the evolutionary relationships between Fagara articulata and other plants within the same family and genus.
uses: Fagara articulata is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In some cultures, its roots and bark are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other conditions. Additionally, it is said to have antiseptic and antifungal properties, which aid in wound healing and skin conditions. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Fagara articulata for medicinal purposes.

side_effects: Fagara articulata, also known as the West African bitter spice, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. However, there is limited information available on its side effects.
